steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content:
The decisive phrase is “But some scholars also argue that recovering juvenilia by lesser-known writers is essential to understanding literary history”.

This contrast word (but) introduces the second major reason scholars value juvenilia: their role in recovering overlooked contributions to literary history.

The first sentence explains interest in juvenilia for artistic development, while the second introduces a distinct, equally important purpose.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content:
The correct answer captures the dual focus: both “famous authors’ artistic development” and “understanding literary history via lesser-known works”.

The other choices either miss both reasons (C: comparisons), focus on an irrelevant challenge (A: publication barriers), or misrepresent the text’s focus by pitting specific authors against each other (D).

That confirms B is correct.
